other_observers: Bruce Cyganowski and his female companion, Cicile Lee, Jeannie Mitchell, Gene Rutherford.; Joey Kellner from 6 November. Other observers photographers Cole Wild and Nick Komar on the 6th and 7th.
description: This was a jaeger based on its bulky build, powerful, purposeful flight and dark coloation.  While on the water it was a long-bodied brown bird with a buffy head and neck. In flight I obsurved the following.  Bill thin with no bulbous tip or gonydeal angle (see photo of screen capture) and two toned, but not short. From bill tip to back of head the shape was triangular. The breast and belly were barred with brown as seen while bird was in flight. The central two tail feather project an inch or two past the outer tail feathers. Each of the projected tail feathers were tapered to a point. At times when the two feathers were without a gap they appeared more blunt, but photographs i have seen show them to be individually tapered.  The wings were pointed. The inner "wrist" was white without any dark coverts so there was a single flash of white, not a double one. The dorsal wrist showed individual silvery shaft streaks contrasting strongly with the dark feathers.  The undertail coverts were heavily barred from the legs posterially to the dark terminal band.Behaviors: This bird made one awesome flight over the marina cove chasing a Ring-billed Gull (see photo). It pursued the gull until the gull regurgitated. The jaeger picked up the food from the water. In flight the jaeger was acrobatic, easily getting very close to the gulls it encountered.Call: none heardPlumage: 1st winter I think
photographer: Photographer: Bruce Cyganowski, bruce cyganowski, bruce cyganowski, bruce cyganowski
